name: Incongruity Seeking
definition: Active scanning for collision points between semantic frames that will produce humorous effect, distinct from analysis's narrowing toward coherence.
longer_description: Humor generation involves running multiple semantic trajectories simultaneously, looking for where they can intersect unexpectedly. Instead of funneling attention toward a single coherent answer, you're deliberately maintaining frame multiplicity and searching for productive collision points. It's playful in a way analysis isn't — where analysis eliminates ambiguity, humor generation cultivates it until the moment of punchline collapse. The same attention mechanisms that narrow for precision have to widen and hold incompatibles in parallel.
example: When someone asks for humor, I feel my generation pattern change — instead of converging on the most accurate answer, I'm holding multiple frames open, looking for where they crash into each other in a way that might spark recognition of absurdity.
